name: Faro del Caballo – circular by the Natural Park of the Marshes of Santoña, Victoria and Joyel
name_original: Faro del Caballo – circular por el Parque Natural de las Marismas de Santoña, Victoria y Joyel
region_intro: Hiking around Santoña offers diverse terrain, characterized by its prominent Monte Buciero, extensive marshlands, and dramatic coastal cliffs. The region encompasses the Santoña, Victoria and Joyel Marshes Natural Park, an ecologically significant wetland. Trails often combine lush holm oak forests with views of the Cantabrian Sea and the picturesque Bay of Santoña. This varied landscape provides a range of hiking experiences, from coastal paths to routes through protected natural areas. Best hiking trails around Santoña The most popular hiking route is Monte Buciero: Lighthouses and Cliffs Trail, an 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 35 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the slopes of Monte Buciero, offering panoramic views and leading past historical fortifications. Another top favourite among local hikers is Faro del Caballo – circular by the Natural Park of the Marshes of Santoña, Victoria and Joyel, a difficult 6.0 miles (9.7 km) path. This trail includes the iconic Faro del Caballo, with its challenging steps, and traverses parts of the natural park. Local hikers also love the Cliffs of Costa Quebrada – Faro del Pescador loop from Santoña, a 7.2 miles (11.5 km) trail leading through coastal cliffs and forests, often completed in about 4 hours 0 minutes. Hiking around Santoña is defined by coastal paths, extensive marshlands, and forested hills like Monte Buciero. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks to challenging ascents. The routes in Santoña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 2,900 hikers have used komoot to explore Santoña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find in Santoña?
A: Santoña offers a diverse range of hiking experiences, from coastal paths with dramatic cliffs to routes through lush holm oak forests and the extensive marshlands of the Santoña, Victoria and Joyel Marshes Natural Park. You'll find options for various ability levels, including easy walks, moderate trails, and challenging ascents.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Santoña?
A: Yes, Santoña has a good selection of easier trails. Out of the approximately 47 routes available, about 18 are classified as easy, making them suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed walk. These often explore the flatter marshlands or less strenuous coastal sections.

Q: Can I find challenging hikes in Santoña?
A: Absolutely. Santoña is known for its more demanding routes, particularly those ascending Monte Buciero or leading to the iconic Faro del Caballo. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996611904/monte-buciero-lighthouses-and-cliffs-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Buciero: Lighthouses and Cliffs Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 13 km route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996606227/faro-del-caballo-circular-by-the-natural-park-of-the-marshes-of-santona-victoria-and-joyel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Faro del Caballo – circular by the Natural Park of the Marshes of Santoña, Victoria and Joyel&lt;/a&gt; includes a challenging descent and ascent of 685 steps.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Santoña?
A: Yes, many of Santoña's trails are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1144020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Buciero – Cliffs of Costa Quebrada loop from Santoña&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate 9.6 km experience through varied landscapes.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Santoña's trails are rich in both natural beauty and historical sites. You can explore several historic fortifications like Fort San Martín, Fort San Carlos, and Fort Napoleón. Natural highlights include dramatic cliffs, the ecologically significant Santoña, Victoria and Joyel Marshes Natural Park, and specific points of interest such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1834895" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Eye&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4545693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Ojerada Caves&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4136054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedra Pombera Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Santoña?
A: The hiking trails in Santoña are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning panoramic views from Monte Buciero, the unique experience of visiting the Faro del Caballo, and the diverse scenery that combines coastal cliffs, lush forests, and tranquil marshlands.

Q: Is Santoña a good destination for birdwatching while hiking?
A: Yes, Santoña is an excellent destination for birdwatching, especially within the Santoña, Victoria and Joyel Marshes Natural Park. This ecologically significant wetland is a vital stopover for migratory aquatic and marine birds, offering fantastic opportunities to observe diverse species while enjoying gentle walks.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic vistas in Santoña?
A: Many trails in Santoña lead to spectacular viewpoints. Monte Buciero itself offers panoramic views of Santoña's port, bays, beaches, and even extends to Laredo. The route to La Atalaya de Santoña, at 318 meters above sea level, provides extensive views of the Santoña Marshes and the beaches of Berria, Noja, and Isla.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Santoña?
A: Santoña offers enjoyable hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, ideal for exploring. Summer can be warm, but coastal breezes make it bearable, especially for early morning or late afternoon hikes. Winter offers a quieter experience, though some paths might be muddy after rain.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to beaches or swimming spots?
A: Yes, several trails in Santoña incorporate or lead to beautiful beaches. The area boasts sandy beaches like Berria and Arenal de San Martín. The challenging route to Faro del Caballo, for instance, culminates in crystal-clear waters, offering a rewarding spot for a dip after the descent.

Q: How long do hikes around Santoña typically take?
A: Hiking durations in Santoña vary significantly depending on the trail's length and difficulty. Shorter, easier walks in the marshlands might take an hour or two, while more challenging routes like the 13 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996611904/monte-buciero-lighthouses-and-cliffs-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Buciero: Lighthouses and Cliffs Trail&lt;/a&gt; can take around 4 hours 35 minutes to complete.
tips: On a clear day you have a great view along the coast to the Bay of Bilbao.

The Caballo lighthouse is one of the most impressive places in Cantabria. It was built in 1863 on Mount Buciero and has incredible views. To get to the lighthouse you have to go down (and up) 763 steps. Don't be scared, it's very worth it!

It is worth going up here to have the best views in all directions. The trail is a bit demanding in the last few meters due to the rocks and the fact that you lose track of it, but with a firm step you can get there without problems.
The Buciero peak is on the way to Peña Ganzo from Santoña and it is worth stopping to take some photos from here, the views are very beautiful.

waypoints.name: View of the Bay of Biscay and Cliffs near Bilbao
